intro: Racosperma bulgaense, also known as Acacia bulga, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae, native to eastern Australia. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
